= DCF Valuation Specification
Sarah Chen <sarah.chen@acme.example>
2026-05-04: revision 1.2
:doctype: article
:toc:
:icons: font

This document is the formal technical specification for the discounted cash flow methodology used by the ACME Corporate Strategy & FP&A team. It accompanies the working notes in link:dcf-framework.md[the DCF framework markdown note]; this version is the team-wide reference.

== Scope

The methodology covers single-company valuation of publicly traded mid-cap industrial manufacturers. It does not cover:

* Pre-revenue or pre-profitability companies (need separate venture methodology)
* Financial institutions (different cash flow architecture)
* Real estate or infrastructure (need separate cap rate methodology)

For competitor analysis the methodology has been applied to link:../companies/acme-corp.md[ACME] and link:../companies/betawidgets-inc.md[BetaWidgets] as canonical examples.

== Inputs

=== Required inputs per model

Every valuation must specify:

. Forecast horizon: integer years, default 10
. Discount rate: percentage, default WACC per company
. Terminal growth rate: percentage, default 2.0%
. Tax rate: effective rate, default 23%
. Capital structure: debt/equity target weights

=== Optional inputs

. Mid-cycle exit multiple (EBITDA basis) — only if running exit-multiple cross-check
. Currency override (defaults to USD)
. Segment-level inputs — only if sum-of-parts valuation

== Calculation procedure

[NOTE]
====
The procedure below is normative. Deviations must be documented in the company's individual model with rationale.
====

=== Step 1 — Build explicit forecast

For each year in the forecast horizon, project:

[source]
----
Revenue
- COGS
= Gross Profit
- SG&A
- R&D
- D&A
= EBIT
- Cash taxes (EBIT * tax_rate)
+ D&A
- Maintenance capex
- Change in working capital
= Free Cash Flow to Firm (FCFF)
----

The forecast is bottoms-up by segment where possible. For ACME this means separate revenue and margin projections for Widget Distribution and Precision Components.

=== Step 2 — Terminal value

Two methods, primary plus cross-check.

==== Perpetuity growth method (primary)

[source]
----
TV = FCFF_(year_n+1) / (WACC - g)
----

Where `g` is the terminal growth rate (typically 2.0%) and `WACC` is the discount rate used in the explicit forecast.

==== Exit multiple method (cross-check)

[source]
----
TV = year_n_EBITDA * mid_cycle_exit_multiple
----

The exit multiple MUST be mid-cycle, not current trading multiple. Past mistakes have come from using current multiples that subsequently mean-reverted (see link:../companies/acme-corp.md[ACME historical mistake reference]).

If the two terminal value methods diverge by more than 20%, the explicit forecast must be reviewed before proceeding.

=== Step 3 — Discount to present value

[source]
----
PV = sum_(year=1)^n FCFF_year / (1 + WACC)^year + TV / (1 + WACC)^n
----

=== Step 4 — Bridge to equity value

[source]
----
Equity Value = Enterprise Value - Net Debt - Other adjustments (pension, minority interest)
Fair value per share = Equity Value / Diluted shares outstanding
----

== Sensitivity analysis

[IMPORTANT]
====
Every published fair value MUST be accompanied by a sensitivity matrix on (WACC, terminal growth). Single point estimates are misleading and not permitted in team deliverables.
====

Standard sensitivity ranges:

* WACC: -100bps, base, +100bps (three values)
* Terminal growth: -50bps, base, +50bps (three values)

This produces a 3x3 = 9-cell fair value matrix. The team's bull/base/bear case framing derives from this matrix:

* Bear case = (WACC +100bps, terminal growth -50bps) cell
* Base case = (WACC base, terminal growth base) cell
* Bull case = (WACC -100bps, terminal growth +50bps) cell

== Sum-of-parts valuation

Applicable when a company has segments with materially different growth and risk profiles. For ACME this is the case (Widget Distribution + Precision Components, see link:../companies/acme-corp.md[ACME company note]).

Procedure:

. Build separate explicit forecasts for each segment
. Apply segment-appropriate exit multiples (NOT a single company multiple)
. Sum segment enterprise values to get total enterprise value
. Bridge to equity value as in step 4 above

== Approval workflow

. Author drafts model and methodology note
. Peer reviewer (typically link:../people/dana-liu.md[Dana Liu] for industrials) reviews assumptions
. Team director (link:../people/yuki-tanaka.md[Yuki Tanaka]) approves before external publication
. Material methodology changes require quarterly review signoff
